checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2674800000 (2010-09-01 to 2010-11-30) declining to SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2214300000 (2010-12-01 to 2011-02-28), a -17.2% drop, while ReceivablesNetCurrent=651000000 (2010-11-30) rose to ReceivablesNetCurrent=777900000 (2011-02-28), a +19.5% increase
evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2011-03-31, balance sheet and income statement data

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=1763200000 (2010-05-31) grew to Goodwill=1828800000 (2011-02-28), +3.7%, versus PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=5465600000 (2010-05-31) growing to PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=6330300000 (2011-02-28), +15.8%

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=172400000 (2009-06-01 to 2009-08-31) and NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=556200000 (2010-06-01 to 2010-08-31) are the only two operating cash flow data points provided; no OCF figures exist for the Nov-2010 or Feb-2011 quarters despite Net Income spiking to NetIncomeLoss=1025600000 (2010-09-01 to 2010-11-30)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margin expanded from GrossProfit=504700000/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2188300000 (23.1%, 2010-06-01 to 2010-08-31) to GrossProfit=768300000/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2674800000 (28.7%, 2010-09-01 to 2010-11-30) to GrossProfit=853600000/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2214300000 (38.6%, 2010-12-01 to 2011-02-28), even as revenue itself declined in the final quarter
InventoryNet=816900000 (2010-11-30) rose sharply to InventoryNet=1133600000 (2011-02-28), a +38.8% increase, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet fell from 2674800000 to 2214300000 over the same period

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss swung sharply from NetIncomeLoss=297700000 (2010-06-01 to 2010-08-31) to NetIncomeLoss=1025600000 (2010-09-01 to 2010-11-30, +244%) to NetIncomeLoss=542100000 (2010-12-01 to 2011-02-28, -47%), far more volatile than the corresponding revenue changes of +22%, +22%, and -17% respectively (SalesRevenueGoodsNet=2188300000, 2674800000, 2214300000)

In the 2010-09-01 to 2010-11-30 quarter, NetIncomeLoss=1025600000 exceeded OperatingIncomeLoss=658200000 by roughly $367 million, unlike surrounding quarters where OperatingIncomeLoss=770800000 exceeded NetIncomeLoss=542100000 (2010-12-01 to 2011-02-28) and OperatingIncomeLoss=410300000 exceeded NetIncomeLoss=297700000 (2010-06-01 to 2010-08-31), suggesting a large non-operating item drove that quarter's earnings

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=552500000 (2010-05-31) grew to AccountsPayableCurrent=645400000 (2011-02-28), +16.8%, and AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent remained in the 605400000-681000000 range across the period, roughly tracking business activity

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Q/A filed 2010-10-29 (accession 0001193125-10-240904) amending the prior 10-Q for the quarter ended 2010-08-31, within the review window
Historical late-filing indicators include NT 10-Q filed 2005-01-14, NT 10-Q filed 2007-01-10, and NT 10-K filed 2007-07-31, showing a pattern of filing delays earlier in the company's history

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly data points span only from 2009-06-01 to 2011-02-28 (roughly two fiscal years of quarterly figures from 10-Q/10-Q/A filings), with no full annual 10-K income statement/balance sheet series provided, and operating cash flow data limited to only two quarters (NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities for 2009-08-31 and 2010-08-31 periods only)
evidence.location: Aggregated financial_series_point_in_time coverage across provided 10-Q/10-Q/A filings

top_signals: Net income (NetIncomeLoss=1025600000, 2010-09-01 to 2010-11-30) exceeded operating income (OperatingIncomeLoss=658200000) by ~$367M in a single quarter, an inversion of the normal pattern seen in adjacent quarters, suggesting a large non-operating/non-recurring gain drove earnings well beyond core operating performance and revenue growth (revenue +22% vs NI +244% QoQ)

Receivables grew +19.5% (ReceivablesNetCurrent 651000000 to 777900000) while revenue fell -17.2% (SalesRevenueGoodsNet 2674800000 to 2214300000) in the Nov-2010 to Feb-2011 quarter, and inventory jumped +38.8% (InventoryNet 816900000 to 1133600000) over the same period of declining sales

Gross margin expanded sharply and steadily from 23.1% to 28.7% to 38.6% over three consecutive quarters (GrossProfit/SalesRevenueGoodsNet), a magnitude of expansion that, combined with the revenue decline in the final quarter, warrants scrutiny though it is plausibly explained by rising fertilizer commodity prices during this period
A 10-Q/A amendment was filed on 2010-10-29 (accession 0001193125-10-240904) for the quarter ended 2010-08-31, indicating a correction during the review window

Data coverage is limited to roughly two fiscal years of quarterly figures with sparse operating cash flow data (only 2 of 6 quarters), constraining the ability to fully validate trends across multiple checklist items
